title: Hooks, Configuration, Credentials, and Repository Trust · Git Stakeholder Mission
summary: A regulated product release board. The board needs reviewable stacked changes, verified release identity, pinned submodules, sparse developer workflows, byte-stable content policy, and defense against untrusted repository execution. Planned artifact: Deliver Scaled and Trusted Multi-Repository Release with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: Define authorship, signoff, review, update, merge, rewrite, rollback, deletion, archival, and exception rules with observable enforcement.
Bind reviewed source, verified tag target, clean reproducible build, artifact digest, attestation, publication record, and rollback ref.
Treat URLs, protocols, hooks, recursive commands, credentials, relative paths, and untrusted nested code as explicit trust boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
project case — Case G29 is a project in a disposable repository. conditional user configuration injects a credential helper and hooks path for this repository. The learner must predict scope and origin identify the exact protected or local source of each effective value, run git config --show-origin --show-scope --get-regexp "^(core|credential|include|safe)\.", explain a global includeIf sets credential.helper while local core.hooksPath points into repository data, test this changed condition: change repository ownership and refuse a wildcard safe.directory exception, and preserve this recovery boundary: capture effective config, remove narrow trust grants, and rotate any exposed credential.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define authorship, signoff, review, update, merge, rewrite, rollback, deletion, archival, and exception rules with observable enforcement.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-untrusted-repository-defense-c4exw6" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Untrusted Repository Defense into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-untrusted-repository-defense-c4exw6
# scenario-key: trace-c4exw6 probe-6wxe4c boundary-exw6c4 owner-c4exw6x fault-c4exw6z
# before-ref: main=a11cec4 candidate=b00b1w6
# hypothesis: conditional user configuration injects a credential helper and hooks path for this repository
git config --show-origin --show-scope --get-regexp "^(core|credential|include|safe)\."
# expected: scope and origin identify the exact protected or local source of each effective value
# observation: a global includeIf sets credential.helper while local core.hooksPath points into repository data
# changed-case: change repository ownership and refuse a wildcard safe.directory exception
# after-ref: main=a11cec4 candidate=c0ffee6
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: capture effective config, remove narrow trust grants, and rotate any exposed credential
# transfer-limit: inspect unknown repositories in isolation with hooks, filters, aliases, and external helpers disabled
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect untrusted repositories as data in an isolated environment; disable execution paths, verify objects and config, and grant trust narrowly and reversibly.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, apply Gita Untrusted Repository Defense without its stated preconditions, omit the repository trust and local automation threat model trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Gita Untrusted Repository Defense for repository trust and local automation threat model.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Gita Untrusted Repository Defense condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Gita Untrusted Repository Defense output and hide the violated repository trust and local automation threat model assumption instead of explaining the failure. This does not resolve test an acceptance boundary.

steps.title: Defend the stakeholder delivery
steps.instruction: Write the project evidence note for Gita Untrusted Repository Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G38 is a project in a disposable repository. conditional user configuration injects a credential helper and hooks path for this repository. The learner must predict scope and origin identify the exact protected or local source of each effective value, run git config --show-origin --show-scope --get-regexp "^(core|credential|include|safe)\.", explain a global includeIf sets credential.helper while local core.hooksPath points into repository data, test this changed condition: change repository ownership and refuse a wildcard safe.directory exception, and preserve this recovery boundary: capture effective config, remove narrow trust grants, and rotate any exposed credential. Competency probe: Inspect untrusted repositories as data in an isolated environment; disable execution paths, verify objects and config, and grant trust narrowly and reversibly.
steps.why: Retrieving Gita Untrusted Repository Defense through the concrete repository trust and local automation threat model case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Inspect untrusted repositories as data in an isolated environment; disable execution paths, verify objects and config, and grant trust narrowly and reversibly.

hints: Name the Gita Untrusted Repository Defense precondition or invariant that the project case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G38 is a project in a disposable repository. conditional user configuration injects a credential helper and hooks path for this repository. The learner must predict scope and origin identify the exact protected or local source of each effective value, run git config --show-origin --show-scope --get-regexp "^(core|credential|include|safe)\.", explain a global includeIf sets credential.helper while local core.hooksPath points into repository data, test this changed condition: change repository ownership and refuse a wildcard safe.directory exception, and preserve this recovery boundary: capture effective config, remove narrow trust grants, and rotate any exposed credential. Competency probe: Inspect untrusted repositories as data in an isolated environment; disable execution paths, verify objects and config, and grant trust narrowly and reversibly.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Git never executes repository-controlled programs until after the user explicitly runs a build.
